name: Roasted Garlic
recipeInstructions:
1 Preheat oven to 375 degrees F (190 degrees C).
2 Remove the papery outer covering of whole garlic head, but do not separate the cloves or peel them.
3 Place as many whole heads of garlic on a large square of heavy-duty aluminum foil as there are people to be served. Fold up foil so that the cloves are completely wrapped.
4 Bake in the preheated oven for 1 hour and 15 minutes.
5 Serve each diner a head of garlic and some bread and butter. Separate cloves.
6 Hold a clove over a piece of buttered bread and squeeze gently until the garlic puree pops out, like toothpaste from a tube.
7 Spread and eat.
Garlic Puree:
1 To make a batch of Garlic Puree for later use, let roasted heads of garlic cool, unwrapped for at least 5 minutes.
2 Gently separate the cloves and squeeze each one over a fine-meshed sieve so that the softened garlic pops into the sieve.
3 With a wooden spatula or wooden spoon, rub garlic through the sieve into a small container or bowl.
4 Cover tightly with plastic wrap and refrigerate the puree if needed.
Quick Puree:
1 If you are in a hurry, skip the refinement of the sieve. Simply squeeze the cloves, one by one, over a bowl.
2 When all the cloves have been squeezed, use a rubber spatula to push the puree into a neat mound.
3 Cover tightly with plastic wrap and refrigerate until needed. It will keep for months. To keep indefinitely, cover with a film of olive oil.
